title: Preflight safety check requests are being billed against tenant quota and causing 429s for interactive traffic
preview: Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload where each interactive chat request is preceded by a lightweight 'preflight' safety/scoring check. After enabling a preflight microservice, FinSight started seeing persistent 429 throttles for low-latency interactive chat traffic. Impact: end-users get immediate 429s for...
text:
Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload where each interactive chat request is preceded by a lightweight 'preflight' safety/scoring check. After enabling a preflight microservice, FinSight started seeing persistent 429 throttles for low-latency interactive chat traffic. Impact: end-users get immediate 429s for short chat requests despite overall tenant usage being within expected bounds. The customer reports that each chat interaction produces two quota decrements in the console: one for preflight and one for the actual generation.

Expected behavior: preflight checks are lightweight and should either be exempt from tenant token accounting (internal preflight) or billed at a small fixed cost, not double-counting the real generation request. Observed behavior: preflight + generation both decrement tenant token buckets as full requests, leading to tenant quota exhaustion and 429s ...

title: SDK retry storm + small connection pool causing serialized streaming requests and intermittent inference timeouts
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a recent regression in perceived inference latency and intermittent request timeouts for streaming responses. The customer is on a Dedicated deployment and uses our official Go and Python SDKs behind an internal gateway. Impact: API calls that previously returned streaming tokens with...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a recent regression in perceived inference latency and intermittent request timeouts for streaming responses. The customer is on a Dedicated deployment and uses our official Go and Python SDKs behind an internal gateway. Impact: API calls that previously returned streaming tokens within ~120–200ms now see long tails up to 6–10s or outright 504/ETIMEDOUT for some requests.

Observed behavior:
- Increased end-to-end latency for streaming calls from a subset of customer hosts beginning 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC.
- Spikes of 429/502/504 correlated with client-side retries and connection churn.
- Host-level connection pools show active connections ~= configured max_connections_per_host, while requests queue on the client.

Expected behavior:
- Streaming responses begin delivering tokens within expected latency envelope; client retries/backoff should not serialize...

title: High attach latency and throughput collapse from small-chunk inference stream CQ saturation
preview: Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach ti...
text:
Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach timeouts and increased fallbacks to smaller model variants. Environment: prod us-west dedicated pool, heterogeneous GPU hosts (A100 80GB + A10 mixed), customer traffic characterized by many short inference streams (~12-32 tokens) at high qps.

We observed a pattern inconsistent with normal autoscaler signals: autoscaler did not trigger additional hosts despite attach latency rising. Support triaged as potential noisy-tenant + kernel-level IO backpressure (PCIe completion queue saturation) affecting attach path and the serving scheduler fairness.

Impact details: Customer repo...

title: Post-migration edge proxy: intermittent 429s for mixed API keys and signed-url callbacks
preview:
Issue summary:
After a scheduled edge proxy migration (blue->green) on 2026-03-09, the customer reports intermittent 429 responses for a subset of traffic that uses a mix of persistent API keys and signed URL callbacks. Impact: several ETL workers and webhook callbacks saw periodic failures for ~20 minutes during a ...
text:
Issue summary:
After a scheduled edge proxy migration (blue->green) on 2026-03-09, the customer reports intermittent 429 responses for a subset of traffic that uses a mix of persistent API keys and signed URL callbacks. Impact: several ETL workers and webhook callbacks saw periodic failures for ~20 minutes during a heavy ingest window, causing backlog and retries downstream.

Impact:
- Acme Analytics batch ingest pipeline experienced ~18% failed request rate during the window, causing requeues and delayed downstream processing.
- Customer reported billing and operational disruption; they are on a Dedicated plan and requested immediate assistance.

Environment:
- Customer Dedicated pool in us-west-2, routed through new edge-proxy cluster (ep-usw-3).
- Mix of long-lived API keys (service accounts) and short-lived signed URLs generated by their ingestion service.
- HTTP/2 multiplexing be...

title: Batcher synchronization glitch causing periodic p95/p99 latency surges and intermittent 504s for short streaming requests
preview: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours NimbleDocs (dedicated customer) has reported recurring p95/p99 latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts affecting short streaming requests. These occur in bursts approximately every 5-8 minutes and correlate with a rise in batcher flush latency on a subset of nodes. Imp...
text: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours NimbleDocs (dedicated customer) has reported recurring p95/p99 latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts affecting short streaming requests. These occur in bursts approximately every 5-8 minutes and correlate with a rise in batcher flush latency on a subset of nodes. Impact: Customer reports degraded UX for their chat widget during peak hours; some requests hit 504 and are retried by clients. Frequency: spikes observed ~6–12x per hour during the incident window. Initial hypothesis: a synchronization edge-case in the continuous batcher interacting with KV cache warmup/prefetch locks under low-concurrency short-stream traffic.

title: Intermittent 5xx wave from TLS-resume-ticket exhaustion combined with HTTP/2 negotiation mismatch between API gateway and runtime
preview: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NimbusHealth observed repeated 5xx errors for short periods (~2-8 minutes) across multiple chat and embedding routes. The API gateway returned 502/503 and upstream serving runtimes reported rapid connection resets and ephemeral worker evictions. Impact: customer-facing re...
text: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NimbusHealth observed repeated 5xx errors for short periods (~2-8 minutes) across multiple chat and embedding routes. The API gateway returned 502/503 and upstream serving runtimes reported rapid connection resets and ephemeral worker evictions. Impact: customer-facing requests intermittently failed (up to 18% error rate during the peak window) causing degraded application experience for their enterprise workflows. Environment: production (us-east), mixed-hosted and dedicated tenant; incident coincided with a tenant priority reconfiguration from NimbusHealth account team.

title: Intermittent console 504 / aborted uploads: gRPC-web upload stream resets behind customer ADC that coalesces HTTP/2 SETTINGS frames
preview: Issue summary: Intermittent upload failures and 504s observed in Redwood Console for a Private deployment. Failures reproduce only when the user's traffic flows through their on-prem ADC which performs HTTP/2/TCP optimizations. Impact: uploads fail to complete, leading to customer inability to persist prompt dataset...
text: Issue summary: Intermittent upload failures and 504s observed in Redwood Console for a Private deployment. Failures reproduce only when the user's traffic flows through their on-prem ADC which performs HTTP/2/TCP optimizations. Impact: uploads fail to complete, leading to customer inability to persist prompt datasets and occasional console timeouts; affects a subset of users behind the ADC, not all tenants.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer CurrentsAI reported a sudden spike of 503/5xx responses starting 2026-03-10 02:10 UTC for a subset of their requests. Error rate peaked ~18% for the tenant for ~45 minutes.

Impact:
- Tenant-facing latency increased and many streaming sessions were terminated with 503.
- Several serving-runtime pods experienced OOM kills and restarted; autoscaler reacted by adding nodes which increased churn.
- Customer reports multiple failed inference calls and degraded UX for production users.

Timeline (high-level):
- 02:08 UTC: tenant traffic shows a shift: 90th/99th prompt length jumped from ~400 tokens to ~6k tokens (long-tail burst from batch import job).
- 02:10 UTC: API Gateway backlog increases, 503s observed and routed to support alerts.
- 02:15 - 02:40 UTC: multiple runtime pods report GPU memory fragmentation and OOM; KV cache evictions spike.
- 02:45 UTC: mitigat...

text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports repeated 429s during a sustained ingest job that uses multiplexed HTTP/2 clients and short-lived signed URLs. They see higher-than-expected throttle counts when reusing the same underlying connection across several ephemeral API keys and signed callbacks.

Impact:
- Intermittent 429s on 3 of 5 ingest workers, causing job retries and delayed backfill.
- Customer on a Dedicated pool; affects SLA and daily throughput.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (prod-us-west) pinned to redwood/open-llama-13b; autoscaling disabled.
- Ingress: client uses HTTP/2 connection reuse with multiple multiplexed streams, each stream attaching a freshly requested signed URL token per upload chunk.

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Open a single persistent HTTP/2 connection from an orchestrator to our edge.
2) Spawn 20 concurrent streams over that connection.
3) For e...

text:
Customer reports streaming outputs occasionally terminate early with no explicit error. They receive an apparently clean end-of-stream but the assistant text is truncated relative to expected completion length.

Asks to customer:

title: SDK exponential backoff jitter misconfiguration causing serialized retries and apparent inference timeouts for bulk requests
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a spike in end-to-end inference latency and client-side timeouts when sending large batched inference requests (bulk job of ~25 concurrent requests per client). The SDK appears to serialize retries and produces long head-of-line delays rather than distributing retry attempts across he...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a spike in end-to-end inference latency and client-side timeouts when sending large batched inference requests (bulk job of ~25 concurrent requests per client). The SDK appears to serialize retries and produces long head-of-line delays rather than distributing retry attempts across healthy connections.

Impact:
- Several customers on Dedicated capacity experienced perceived timeouts and increased median latency for batched/parallel inference.
- Client-side metrics show heavy retry activity and a single active TCP connection per SDK process during peak bursts.
- Affects production workloads for PromptCraft (dedicated allocation).

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (prod us-east-1)
- Customer SDK: redwood-sdk-js v2.3.0 (custom config)
- Load pattern: ~25 parallel connections from a single process batching 16 prompts per request

Observed behavior:
- Burst ...

title: Resume subprotocol mismatch causes session rebind and drops terminal attachment on reconnect
preview:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that after a brief network blip their websocket stream reconnects but the final embedded attachment (base64 PNG) is missing and the assistant sends duplicated system messages. The application sees a successful 101 upgrade on reconnect yet the streamed session does not resume semantics...
text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that after a brief network blip their websocket stream reconnects but the final embedded attachment (base64 PNG) is missing and the assistant sends duplicated system messages. The application sees a successful 101 upgrade on reconnect yet the streamed session does not resume semantics and the last 'attachment' chunk is never emitted.

Impact:
- ParcelFlow's invoice-generation flow fails to attach generated receipt images to the final response for ~2% of streamed requests during the reconnect window.
- End users see duplicated system messages ("System: Session resumed") and a truncated output (missing base64 attachment).

Environment:
- Customer on Dedicated cluster (prod, us-east) behind corporate edge proxy (vendor: Korunner WAF) and an internal ALB.
- SDK: redwood-js v1.4.2 using websocket with sec-websocket-protocol: "redwood-stream-v1" set by client...

title: Cross-proxy normalization of idempotency header causing duplicate tool/function executions
preview: Customer reports repeated duplicate side-effecting tool/function invocations when using function-calling with retries. The customer is on a dedicated plan behind a corporate reverse proxy that normalizes headers (lowercasing, truncation of long header values, and replacing spaces with hyphens). NimbusAI sees duplica...
text: Customer reports repeated duplicate side-effecting tool/function invocations when using function-calling with retries. The customer is on a dedicated plan behind a corporate reverse proxy that normalizes headers (lowercasing, truncation of long header values, and replacing spaces with hyphens). NimbusAI sees duplicate webhook/tool calls on retry even though they supply stable idempotency keys in the SDK. Hypothesis: the proxy is rewriting/persistently normalizing the Idempotency header in-flight, so Redwood's dedupe layer receives a different header value on the retry path and treats it as a new request.

text:
Issue summary
Northstar HR reported elevated tail latency on their Dedicated endpoint during a model canary rollout. The customer observed p95 and p99 latency increases and intermittent client timeouts during peak traffic. The rollout did not automatically pause/rollback, requiring manual intervention by on-call.

Customer impact
- Customer-facing latency degradation for chat completions.
- Intermittent client-side timeouts (HTTP 499s observed from the gateway; no increase in 5xx at the platform edge initially).
- The customer explicitly requested an automated safety rail: SLO-based auto-rollback (or auto-pause) for canary/A-B rollouts.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment
- Regions: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)
- Route: /v1/chat/completions (tenant: northstar-hr)
- Rollout type: canary traffic shift (10% -> 25% planned)

Timeline (all times PT)
- 2026-02-17 09:42: Rollout st...

title: Perf canary alerting false positive on small sample (FP8/INT8 rollout) — add min-sample + improve metric tagging/aggregation
preview:
Issue summary:
Perf canary fired TTFT/TPOT regression alerts for FP8/INT8 canary cohorts that were later determined to be false positives caused by small sample sizes and aggregation gaps (missing/partial dimensions caused baseline + canary to mix in queries).

Impact:
- Triggered an automated rollback in one region...
text:
Issue summary:
Perf canary fired TTFT/TPOT regression alerts for FP8/INT8 canary cohorts that were later determined to be false positives caused by small sample sizes and aggregation gaps (missing/partial dimensions caused baseline + canary to mix in queries).

Impact:
- Triggered an automated rollback in one region cohort and created follow-up noise in #eng-sre / #incidents.
- Slowed down kernel rollout decision-making (teams did extra triage to prove it was noise).
- Risk: could mask real regressions if alert fatigue sets in.

Context:
Ongoing FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking + staged rollout. Canary cohorts are low-volume by design, but current alert rules assume a stable sample size and consistent metric tagging.

Current behavior observed:
- Alert triggered on p95 TTFT when canary cohort had <10 requests in the lookback window.
- A single cold-start / cache-miss request dominated the...

- Region: us-east

Detection:

- SRE saw increased scheduler queue depth for the dedicated namespace and elevated GPU utilization from non-dedicated workloads.

- Noisy-neighbor / capacity...

title: Dedicated: burst capacity not activating during traffic spikes (Northstar Payments, us-east)
preview:
Issue summary
- Customer reports Dedicated burst capacity did not activate during predictable traffic spikes. Throughput capped at baseline replicas, causing queueing and elevated p95/p99 latency.
- Customer expectation: burst should add temporary replicas within 2–5 minutes when utilization/queue depth crosses thre...
text:
Issue summary
- Customer reports Dedicated burst capacity did not activate during predictable traffic spikes. Throughput capped at baseline replicas, causing queueing and elevated p95/p99 latency.
- Customer expectation: burst should add temporary replicas within 2–5 minutes when utilization/queue depth crosses threshold.

Impact
- 2026-02-12 17:05–18:20 UTC: sustained elevated latency and intermittent 5xx from upstream timeouts due to request queue buildup.
- Customer-visible symptoms:
 - Latency increase: p95 from ~850ms to ~4.8s (chat), p99 up to ~9–12s.
 - RPS capped at baseline; queue depth increased and stayed elevated.
 - No visible scale-out events in their Console view.

Customer / Tier
- Northstar Payments (Dedicated)

Environment
- prod, us-east
- Dedicated pool: northstar-prod-gpu-pool (A100-80GB)

What we expected
- Burst capacity should temporarily scale above reserved b...
links: Customer-provided timestamps + request ID sample set (stored in Support drive; not for external sharing)

title: Streaming intermittently stalls for Northwind Robotics (no [DONE], client waits indefinitely)
preview:
Issue summary:
- Customer sees streaming responses stop after some tokens. Connection remains open; no terminal event.

Impact:
- Their in-product assistant gets stuck; they cancel and retry.

Environment:
- Hosted API in eu-west region.
- Using server-sent events over HTTPS via corporate proxy.

Triage notes:
- Met...
text:
Issue summary:
- Customer sees streaming responses stop after some tokens. Connection remains open; no terminal event.

Impact:
- Their in-product assistant gets stuck; they cancel and retry.

Environment:
- Hosted API in eu-west region.
- Using server-sent events over HTTPS via corporate proxy.

Triage notes:
- Metrics show elevated open connections with low egress bytes.
- Customer proxy may buffer small chunks; they reported it improves when disabling proxy or increasing chunk size.

Workaround shared:
- Disable HTTP buffering on proxy (nginx: proxy_buffering off), or use websocket transport (if available), or set client read timeout + retry.

Resolution:
- Not fully reproducible internally; linked to known issue page and provided proxy guidance. Marking closed once customer confirmed workaround reduced occurrences.

title: 5xx spike during customer A/B test rollout; request configurable auto-rollback thresholds + visibility into rollback decisions
preview:

They are requesting:
1) Configurable safety rails for A/B and canary rollouts (auto-pause/rollback based on error-rate and lat...
text:
Customer (Northstar HR) ran an A/B test (baseline model vs candidate quantized variant) on their primary chat completion route and observed a short but sharp increase in HTTP 5xx responses.

They are requesting:
1) Configurable safety rails for A/B and canary rollouts (auto-pause/rollback based on error-rate and latency thresholds).
2) Better visibility into rollout decisions: what triggered a pause/rollback, which monitors were evaluated, what thresholds/windows were applied, and links to the supporting metrics.

Issue Summary
- During an A/B test (approx. 10% candidate traffic), 5xx rate spiked above their internal SLO. Customer manually reverted traffic split to baseline.
- Customer states that existing alerts were not actionable enough during the event because they lacked a clear explanation of which part of the rollout was responsible and what the platform would have done automat...

Intermittent GPU OOMs in Northwind Analytics dedicated pool during high-churn chat traffic (many short-lived sessions + cancellations). OOMs occur despite dashboards showing non-zero free GPU memory prior to failure. Initial hypothesis: KV cache arena fragmentation causing allocation failures / forced request shedding.

- Two pods restarted due to CUDA OOM; transient increased p99 latency (~+1.2s) while pool rebalanced.
- Customer escalated due to concern about stability during upcoming load test.

Customer-reported behavior:
- Failures clustered during bursty load (spikes to 3–4x baseline QPS) with frequent client cancellations (mobile app backgrounding).
- Largest prompts were not the trigger; failures correlated with churn.

Environment:
- Dedicated: nw-...

title: Latency regression after runtime-1.22 canary: elevated p99 decode latency correlated with KV compaction activity
preview:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) an...
text:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) and increased allocator fragmentation ratio oscillation.
- Canary was rolled back (feature flag disabled + cohort drained) per guardrails.

Customer Impact
- Customer: Northwind Analytics (enterprise) reported increased p99 latency and intermittent request timeouts during bursty chat traffic.
- Symptoms: p95 largely stable; p99 degraded (notable tail spikes) during bursts and after churn events (rapid session start/stop).
- Scope: Only canary cohort in us-east-1. Control region (eu-west-1) did not show comparable change.

Detection
- 2026-01-29 09:12 PT: SRE alert fired: host...

text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports they cannot create an API key in the Console. The "Create API key" button appears, but is not clickable / click does nothing.

Impact:
- Blocks activation milestone (API key creation → first request).
- Customer unable to complete onboarding and send first API call.
- Support volume risk during lifecycle growth experiments rollout (console nudges).

Customer context:
- Company: DevFlow AI
- Plan: self-serve Hosted API
- User report: "The onboarding checklist is telling me to create a key, but I can't click the button. It looks like something is on top of it."

Environment:
- Console: app.redwoodinference.com
- Observed in: prod (us-east)
- Browser: Chrome 121 / macOS (also reproduced on Arc)
- Feature flags: lifecycle growth console nudges enabled for cohort (holdout = false)

title: Streaming responses freeze mid-generation for Arcadia Health (SSE stalls, request never completes)

text:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports streaming chat responses intermittently stop mid-generation. Client keeps the HTTP connection open; server stops emitting SSE tokens and never sends [DONE].

Impact:
- Production incident for Arcadia Health’s support-agent. End users see partial completions and stuck spinners.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool in us-east.
- Client uses OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoint.

Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Start streaming completion with max_tokens ~800.
2) After ~10–40 seconds, stream stalls mid-sentence.
3) No further bytes, no close, no error.

Investigation notes:
- Correlates with longer generations + high concurrent streams.
- Gateway shows upstream still open; runtime logs show request context cancelled late or not at all.
- Suspected backpressure interaction between token streamer + gzip + chunked transfer.

Workaround:
- Customer can s...

text:
## Issue summary
Customer (Proxima Bank) reports a sharp increase in HTTP 429 responses starting ~2026-01-20 09:10 PT on /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings. They believe this correlates with Redwood’s recent “priority routing / capacity protection” changes.

- Auth: enterprise API key (account: PROXIMA-ENT-014)

Issue summary
- Customer (Northwind Analytics) reported a throughput drop after initiating a Dedicated pool expansion in us-east (A100). Expansion nodes were provisioned but a high fraction were automatically moved to Quarantined during post-provision validation, leading to a temporary capacity shortfall.

Customer impact

- Observed intermittent request timeouts when concurrency exceeded the reduced active capacity.
- No data loss. Customer has fallback disabled and pins model alias; they could not route to hosted/shared capacity.

text:
Customer reports that the new Console "Route Experiments" results dashboard is not updating (appears stuck on the same deltas/graphs) even though traffic is flowing to both variants.

Issue summary:
- Console results view for an active route experiment shows stale charts/metrics (latency/cost/error deltas unchanged for ~45–90 minutes).
- Customer validated traffic is being split (they see variant headers in responses).

Impact:
- Customer cannot make ramp/stop decisions from Console due to stale guardrail readouts.
- Increased risk of running an experiment longer than intended / missing regression.

Customer context:
- Account: Acme AI (beta design partner)
- Experiment: route-level A/B test (A=baseline, B=model swap) on production route.
- Customer expectations: near-real-time results (they assumed <5–10 min lag).

Issue summary:

Notes:
Customer is pinning model version but sees this when their prompt is near context limit.

text:
Issue summary:
Nordholm Payments reports that after enabling EU-only region pinning for their Hosted API project, they observed at least one request attempting to fall back to a US region when EU capacity was constrained. They require strict enforcement (no cross-region routing/fallback).

Impact:
- Compliance risk perception for customer (regulated payments).
- Customer paused POC traffic until confirmed fixed.

Customer expectation:
- Requests must be served only within the configured allowed regions (EU-only).
- If EU is unavailable/capacity constrained, the request must be blocked with an explicit residency violation error (not retried in US).

Environment:
- Prod
- Customer config: project pinned to EU-only; primary region: eu-west
- Observed behavior: smart router selected eu-west then attempted us-east fallback

Steps to reproduce (from customer):

title: token accounting discrepancy between streaming usage and invoice export
preview:

Customer reports a token accounting discrepancy: the Usage page (streaming chat route) shows ~18% more billable tokens than what they compute from request/response logs. Their invoice export CSV matches the higher number.

text:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a token accounting discrepancy: the Usage page (streaming chat route) shows ~18% more billable tokens than what they compute from request/response logs. Their invoice export CSV matches the higher number.

Impact:
Customer is pausing rollout until they can reconcile token counts for February.

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1) Call /v1/chat/completions with streaming enabled
2) Sum prompt_tokens + completion_tokens from the final chunk they receive
3) Compare to billable tokens shown in Console > Usage and invoice export

Notes:
- Customer claims some requests show completion_tokens=0 in their logs while they still received text
- They suspect retransmits / reconnects

Requested outcome:
Explain what counts as billable tokens for streaming and whether retries can double-count.

title: AuroraMed production surge: multipath fallback + autoscaler deadtime caused session resets and high-tail latency — customer restore plan & post-incident actions
preview: Issue summary: On 2024-11-15 03:12 UTC AuroraMed began seeing elevated tail latency and session resets for multi-turn chat flows. Impact: clients experienced truncated streaming responses, duplicate completions for retrying clients, and an increased 5xx rate (service errors) for approx. 43 minutes. Multiple integrat...
text:
Issue summary: On 2024-11-15 03:12 UTC AuroraMed began seeing elevated tail latency and session resets for multi-turn chat flows. Impact: clients experienced truncated streaming responses, duplicate completions for retrying clients, and an increased 5xx rate (service errors) for approx. 43 minutes. Multiple integrations reported out-of-order tokens and session resumption failures.

Timeline (high level):
- 03:10 UTC: first anomaly flagged by synthetic monitors (latency > 99th pct).
- 03:12 UTC: AuroraMed engineering reported customer-facing errors via support channel and opened an escalation.
- 03:15–03:32 UTC: automatic fallback routing generated multipath requests to variant models; autoscaler entered a deadtime window when scaling signals peaked and could not spin new workers fast enough.
- 03:32 UTC: mitigation step applied (temporary routing throttle and manual scale-up of dedica...
